concerns.text: T022 computes a 'Global Covariance Matrix' across ALL samples to derive a single eigenvalue. This violates Constitution Principle VI and FR-002, which mandate computing the covariance matrix 'for every sample' to derive a per-sample 'entanglement score'. A single global scalar cannot serve as a predictor for sample-specific fidelity loss in the regression model (FR-004).

T030a defines the permutation test against a 'null baseline (predicting mean loss)' to verify MAE improvement. This violates SC-001 and FR-005, which require a permutation test to validate the 'correlation strength (R²) between entanglement features and fidelity loss'. The task conflates model performance significance with feature correlation significance.

T039 implements a 'synthetic fallback' if real data fetch fails. This violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility), which requires 'External datasets MUST be fetched from the same canonical source on every run'. A synthetic fallback introduces non-deterministic data sources for the final analysis, breaking the reproducibility guarantee.

T014 states it will implement 'primary quality dimension' identification 'based on prompt metadata' but does not specify the schema rule or metadata field. This violates FR-003 and SC-004, which require the dimension to be determined 'solely by prompt metadata... independent of model scores' to ensure target independence. Without a defined rule, the implementation cannot guarantee independence.

T042 proposes 'memory-efficient model training' using chunking for Random Forest. This violates the feasibility of the task as `scikit-learn` Random Forest does not support `partial_fit` or incremental training. Implementing this as described will fail or silently degrade to full loading, violating the constraint of running within the 7GB RAM limit if the dataset is large.
